entries.name: /add-dir
entries.description: Add a working directory for file access during the current session. Most `.claude/` configuration is [not discovered](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions#additional-directories-grant-file-access-not-configuration) from the added directory. You can later resume the session from the added directory with `--continue` or `--resume`
entries.searchText: Add a working directory for file access during the current session. Most .claude/ configuration is not discovered from the added directory. You can later resume the session from the added directory with --continue or --resume

entries.name: /advisor
entries.description: Enable or disable the [advisor tool](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/advisor), which consults a second model for guidance at key moments during a task. Accepts `opus`, `sonnet`, `fable` (v2.1.170+), or a full model ID. Without an argument, opens a picker. Requires Claude Code v2.1.98 or later
entries.searchText: Enable or disable the advisor tool, which consults a second model for guidance at key moments during a task. Accepts opus, sonnet, fable (v2.1.170+), or a full model ID. Without an argument, opens a picker. Requires Claude Code v2.1.98 or later

entries.name: /agents
entries.description: Manage [agent](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents) configurations
entries.searchText: Manage agent configurations

entries.name: /autofix-pr
entries.description: Spawn a [Claude Code on the web](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web#auto-fix-pull-requests) session that watches the current branch's PR and pushes fixes when CI fails or reviewers leave comments. Detects the open PR from your checked-out branch with `gh pr view`; to watch a different PR, check out its branch first. By default the cloud session is told to fix every CI failure and review comment; pass a prompt to give it different instructions, for example `/autofix-pr only fix lint and type errors`. Requires the `gh` CLI and access to [Claude Code on the web](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web)
entries.searchText: Spawn a Claude Code on the web session that watches the current branch's PR and pushes fixes when CI fails or reviewers leave comments. Detects the open PR from your checked-out branch with gh pr view; to watch a different PR, check out its branch first. By default the cloud session is told to fix every CI failure and review comment; pass a prompt to give it different instructions, for example /autofix-pr only fix lint and type errors. Requires the gh CLI and access to Claude Code on the web

entries.name: /background
entries.description: Detach the current session to run as a [background agent](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-view) and free this terminal. Pass a prompt to send one more instruction before detaching. Monitor the session with `claude agents`. Alias: `/bg`
entries.searchText: Detach the current session to run as a background agent and free this terminal. Pass a prompt to send one more instruction before detaching. Monitor the session with claude agents. Alias: /bg

entries.name: /batch
entries.description: **[Skill](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#bundled-skills).** Orchestrate large-scale changes across a codebase in parallel. Researches the codebase, decomposes the work into 5 to 30 independent units, and presents a plan. Once approved, spawns one [background subagent](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents#run-subagents-in-foreground-or-background) per unit in an isolated [git worktree](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/worktrees). Each subagent implements its unit, runs tests, and opens a pull request. Requires a git repository. Example: `/batch migrate src/ from Solid to React`
entries.searchText: Skill. Orchestrate large-scale changes across a codebase in parallel. Researches the codebase, decomposes the work into 5 to 30 independent units, and presents a plan. Once approved, spawns one background subagent per unit in an isolated git worktree. Each subagent implements its unit, runs tests, and opens a pull request. Requires a git repository. Example: /batch migrate src/ from Solid to React

entries.name: /branch
entries.description: Create a branch of the current conversation at this point, so you can try a different direction without losing the conversation as it stands. Switches you into the branch and preserves the original, which you can return to with `/resume`. To hand a side task to a background subagent instead of switching into a copy yourself, use `/fork`
entries.searchText: Create a branch of the current conversation at this point, so you can try a different direction without losing the conversation as it stands. Switches you into the branch and preserves the original, which you can return to with /resume. To hand a side task to a background subagent instead of switching into a copy yourself, use /fork

entries.name: /btw
entries.description: Ask a quick [side question](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/interactive-mode#side-questions-with-%2Fbtw) without adding to the conversation
entries.searchText: Ask a quick side question without adding to the conversation

entries.name: /cd
entries.description: Move this session to a new working directory. The conversation's prompt cache is preserved: the new directory's [`CLAUDE.md`](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory) is appended as a message instead of rebuilding the system prompt. The session is relocated to the new directory's project storage, so `--resume` and `--continue` find it from there. Prompts you to trust the directory if you haven't worked in it before. To grant access to an extra directory without moving the session, use `/add-dir`. Restrict or disable `/cd` targets with [`Cd` permission rules](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions#cd). Requires Claude Code v2.1.169 or later; earlier versions report `Unknown command: /cd`
entries.searchText: Move this session to a new working directory. The conversation's prompt cache is preserved: the new directory's CLAUDE.md is appended as a message instead of rebuilding the system prompt. The session is relocated to the new directory's project storage, so --resume and --continue find it from there. Prompts you to trust the directory if you haven't worked in it before. To grant access to an extra directory without moving the session, use /add-dir. Restrict or disable /cd targets with Cd permission rules. Requires Claude Code v2.1.169 or later; earlier versions report Unknown command: /cd

entries.name: /chrome
entries.description: Configure [Claude in Chrome](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/chrome) settings
entries.searchText: Configure Claude in Chrome settings

entries.name: /claude-api
entries.description: **[Skill](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#bundled-skills).** Load Claude API reference material for your project's language (Python, TypeScript, Java, Go, Ruby, C#, PHP, or cURL) and Managed Agents reference. Covers tool use, streaming, batches, structured outputs, and common pitfalls. Also activates automatically when your code imports `anthropic` or `@anthropic-ai/sdk`. Run `/claude-api migrate` to upgrade existing Claude API code to a newer model: Claude asks which files to scan and which model to target, then updates model IDs, thinking configuration, and other parameters that changed between versions. Run `/claude-api managed-agents-onboard` for an interactive walkthrough that creates a new Managed Agent from scratch
entries.searchText: Skill. Load Claude API reference material for your project's language (Python, TypeScript, Java, Go, Ruby, C, PHP, or cURL) and Managed Agents reference. Covers tool use, streaming, batches, structured outputs, and common pitfalls. Also activates automatically when your code imports anthropic or @anthropic-ai/sdk. Run /claude-api migrate to upgrade existing Claude API code to a newer model: Claude asks which files to scan and which model to target, then updates model IDs, thinking configuration, and other parameters that changed between versions. Run /claude-api managed-agents-onboard for an interactive walkthrough that creates a new Managed Agent from scratch

entries.name: /clear
entries.description: Start a new conversation with empty context. The previous conversation stays available in `/resume`. Pass a name to label the previous conversation in the `/resume` picker. To free up context while continuing the same conversation, use `/compact` instead. Aliases: `/reset`, `/new`
entries.searchText: Start a new conversation with empty context. The previous conversation stays available in /resume. Pass a name to label the previous conversation in the /resume picker. To free up context while continuing the same conversation, use /compact instead. Aliases: /reset, /new

entries.name: /code-review
entries.description: **[Skill](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#bundled-skills).** Review the current diff for correctness bugs and for reuse, simplification, and efficiency cleanups. Pass `--fix` to apply findings to your working tree, `--comment` to post them as inline GitHub PR comments, or `ultra` to run a deep [cloud review](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/ultrareview). From v2.1.154, `/simplify` runs a separate cleanup-only review that applies fixes without hunting for bugs. See [Review a diff locally](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/code-review#review-a-diff-locally) for effort levels and targeting
entries.searchText: Skill. Review the current diff for correctness bugs and for reuse, simplification, and efficiency cleanups. Pass --fix to apply findings to your working tree, --comment to post them as inline GitHub PR comments, or ultra to run a deep cloud review. From v2.1.154, /simplify runs a separate cleanup-only review that applies fixes without hunting for bugs. See Review a diff locally for effort levels and targeting

entries.name: /color
entries.description: Set the prompt bar color for the current session. Available colors: `red`, `blue`, `green`, `yellow`, `purple`, `orange`, `pink`, `cyan`. Use `default` to reset, or run with no argument to pick a random color. When [Remote Control](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control) is connected, the color syncs to claude.ai/code
entries.searchText: Set the prompt bar color for the current session. Available colors: red, blue, green, yellow, purple, orange, pink, cyan. Use default to reset, or run with no argument to pick a random color. When Remote Control is connected, the color syncs to claude.ai/code

entries.name: /compact
entries.description: Free up context by summarizing the conversation so far. Optionally pass focus instructions for the summary. See [how compaction handles rules, skills, and memory files](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/context-window#what-survives-compaction)
entries.searchText: Free up context by summarizing the conversation so far. Optionally pass focus instructions for the summary. See how compaction handles rules, skills, and memory files

entries.name: /config
entries.description: Open the [Settings](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings) interface to adjust theme, model, [output style](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/output-styles), and other preferences. From v2.1.181, pass one or more `key=value` pairs to set a setting directly without opening the interface, for example `/config thinking=false`. From v2.1.182, named shorthand keys are also accepted, such as `/config theme=dark` or `/config model=sonnet`. The `key=value` form also works in non-interactive mode (`-p`) and from [Remote Control](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control). Run `/config --help` to list every settable key with its options. Alias: `/settings`
entries.searchText: Open the Settings interface to adjust theme, model, output style, and other preferences. From v2.1.181, pass one or more key=value pairs to set a setting directly without opening the interface, for example /config thinking=false. From v2.1.182, named shorthand keys are also accepted, such as /config theme=dark or /config model=sonnet. The key=value form also works in non-interactive mode (-p) and from Remote Control. Run /config --help to list every settable key with its options. Alias: /settings

entries.name: /context
entries.description: Visualize current context usage as a colored grid. Shows optimization suggestions for context-heavy tools, memory bloat, and capacity warnings. In [fullscreen mode](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/fullscreen) the per-item breakdown is collapsed to keep the grid visible. Pass `all` to expand it
entries.searchText: Visualize current context usage as a colored grid. Shows optimization suggestions for context-heavy tools, memory bloat, and capacity warnings. In fullscreen mode the per-item breakdown is collapsed to keep the grid visible. Pass all to expand it

entries.name: /copy
entries.description: Copy the last assistant response to clipboard. Pass a number `N` to copy the Nth-latest response: `/copy 2` copies the second-to-last. When code blocks are present, shows an interactive picker to select individual blocks or the full response. Press `w` in the picker to write the selection to a file instead of the clipboard, which is useful over SSH
entries.searchText: Copy the last assistant response to clipboard. Pass a number N to copy the Nth-latest response: /copy 2 copies the second-to-last. When code blocks are present, shows an interactive picker to select individual blocks or the full response. Press w in the picker to write the selection to a file instead of the clipboard, which is useful over SSH

entries.name: /cost
entries.description: Alias for `/usage`
entries.searchText: Alias for /usage

entries.name: /debug
entries.description: **[Skill](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#bundled-skills).** Enable debug logging for the current session and troubleshoot issues by reading the session debug log. Debug logging is off by default unless you started with `claude --debug`, so running `/debug` mid-session starts capturing logs from that point forward. Optionally describe the issue to focus the analysis
entries.searchText: Skill. Enable debug logging for the current session and troubleshoot issues by reading the session debug log. Debug logging is off by default unless you started with claude --debug, so running /debug mid-session starts capturing logs from that point forward. Optionally describe the issue to focus the analysis

entries.name: /deep-research
entries.description: **[Workflow](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/workflows#bundled-workflows).** Fan out web searches on a question, fetch and cross-check sources, and synthesize a cited report
entries.searchText: Workflow. Fan out web searches on a question, fetch and cross-check sources, and synthesize a cited report

entries.name: /desktop
entries.description: Continue the current session in the Claude Code Desktop app. Requires macOS or Windows and a Claude subscription. Alias: `/app`
entries.searchText: Continue the current session in the Claude Code Desktop app. Requires macOS or Windows and a Claude subscription. Alias: /app

entries.name: /diff
entries.description: Open an interactive diff viewer showing uncommitted changes and per-turn diffs. Use left/right arrows to switch between the current git diff and individual Claude turns, and up/down to browse files
entries.searchText: Open an interactive diff viewer showing uncommitted changes and per-turn diffs. Use left/right arrows to switch between the current git diff and individual Claude turns, and up/down to browse files

entries.name: /doctor
entries.description: Diagnose and verify your Claude Code installation and settings. Results show with status icons. Press `f` to have Claude fix any reported issues
entries.searchText: Diagnose and verify your Claude Code installation and settings. Results show with status icons. Press f to have Claude fix any reported issues

entries.name: /effort
entries.description: Set the model [effort level](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config#adjust-effort-level). Accepts `low`, `medium`, `high`, `xhigh`, `max`, or `ultracode`; available levels depend on the model, and `max` and `ultracode` are session-only. `ultracode` is a Claude Code setting that combines `xhigh` reasoning with automatic [workflow](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/workflows#let-claude-decide-with-ultracode) orchestration. `auto` resets to the model default. Without an argument, opens an interactive slider; use left and right arrows to pick a level and `Enter` to apply. Takes effect immediately without waiting for the current response to finish
entries.searchText: Set the model effort level. Accepts low, medium, high, xhigh, max, or ultracode; available levels depend on the model, and max and ultracode are session-only. ultracode is a Claude Code setting that combines xhigh reasoning with automatic workflow orchestration. auto resets to the model default. Without an argument, opens an interactive slider; use left and right arrows to pick a level and Enter to apply. Takes effect immediately without waiting for the current response to finish

entries.name: /exit
entries.description: Exit the CLI. In an attached [background session](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-view#attach-to-a-session), this detaches and the session keeps running. Alias: `/quit`
entries.searchText: Exit the CLI. In an attached background session, this detaches and the session keeps running. Alias: /quit

entries.name: /export
entries.description: Export the current conversation as plain text. With a filename, writes directly to that file. Without, opens a dialog to copy to clipboard or save to a file
entries.searchText: Export the current conversation as plain text. With a filename, writes directly to that file. Without, opens a dialog to copy to clipboard or save to a file

entries.name: /fast
entries.description: Toggle [fast mode](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/fast-mode) on or off
entries.searchText: Toggle fast mode on or off

entries.name: /feedback
entries.description: Submit feedback, report a bug, or share your conversation. Aliases: `/bug`, `/share`
entries.searchText: Submit feedback, report a bug, or share your conversation. Aliases: /bug, /share

entries.name: /fewer-permission-prompts
entries.description: **[Skill](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#bundled-skills).** Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project `.claude/settings.json` to reduce permission prompts
entries.searchText: Skill. Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts

entries.name: /focus
entries.description: Toggle the focus view, which shows only your last prompt, a one-line tool-call summary with edit diffstats, and the final response. The selection persists across sessions; set [`viewMode`](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings#available-settings) in settings to override it. Only available in [fullscreen rendering](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/fullscreen)
entries.searchText: Toggle the focus view, which shows only your last prompt, a one-line tool-call summary with edit diffstats, and the final response. The selection persists across sessions; set viewMode in settings to override it. Only available in fullscreen rendering

entries.name: /fork
entries.description: Spawn a [forked subagent](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents#fork-the-current-conversation): a background subagent that inherits the full conversation and works on the directive while you keep going. Its result returns to your conversation when it finishes. To switch into a copy of the conversation yourself, use `/branch`. Before v2.1.161, `/fork` is an alias for `/branch`
entries.searchText: Spawn a forked subagent: a background subagent that inherits the full conversation and works on the directive while you keep going. Its result returns to your conversation when it finishes. To switch into a copy of the conversation yourself, use /branch. Before v2.1.161, /fork is an alias for /branch

entries.name: /goal
entries.description: Set a [goal](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/goal): Claude keeps working across turns until the condition is met. With no argument, shows the current or most recently achieved goal. `clear`, `stop`, `off`, `reset`, `none`, or `cancel` removes an active goal early
entries.searchText: Set a goal: Claude keeps working across turns until the condition is met. With no argument, shows the current or most recently achieved goal. clear, stop, off, reset, none, or cancel removes an active goal early

entries.name: /heapdump
entries.description: Write a JavaScript heap snapshot and a memory breakdown to `~/Desktop`, or your home directory on Linux without a Desktop folder, for diagnosing high memory usage. See [troubleshooting](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/troubleshooting#high-cpu-or-memory-usage)
entries.searchText: Write a JavaScript heap snapshot and a memory breakdown to ~/Desktop, or your home directory on Linux without a Desktop folder, for diagnosing high memory usage. See troubleshooting

entries.name: /help
entries.description: Show help and available commands
entries.searchText: Show help and available commands

entries.name: /hooks
entries.description: View [hook](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks) configurations for tool events
entries.searchText: View hook configurations for tool events

entries.name: /ide
entries.description: Manage IDE integrations and show status
entries.searchText: Manage IDE integrations and show status

entries.name: /init
entries.description: Initialize project with a `CLAUDE.md` guide. Set `CLAUDE_CODE_NEW_INIT=1` for an interactive flow that also walks through skills, hooks, and personal memory files
entries.searchText: Initialize project with a CLAUDE.md guide. Set CLAUDECODENEWINIT=1 for an interactive flow that also walks through skills, hooks, and personal memory files

entries.name: /insights
entries.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions, including project areas, interaction patterns, and friction points
entries.searchText: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions, including project areas, interaction patterns, and friction points

entries.name: /install-github-app
entries.description: Set up the [Claude GitHub Actions](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/github-actions) app for a repository. Walks you through selecting a repo and configuring the integration
entries.searchText: Set up the Claude GitHub Actions app for a repository. Walks you through selecting a repo and configuring the integration

entries.name: /install-slack-app
entries.description: Install the Claude Slack app. Opens a browser to complete the OAuth flow
entries.searchText: Install the Claude Slack app. Opens a browser to complete the OAuth flow

entries.name: /keybindings
entries.description: Open your [keyboard shortcuts](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/keybindings) file
entries.searchText: Open your keyboard shortcuts file

entries.name: /login
entries.description: Sign in to your Anthropic account
entries.searchText: Sign in to your Anthropic account

entries.name: /logout
entries.description: Sign out from your Anthropic account
entries.searchText: Sign out from your Anthropic account

entries.name: /loop
entries.description: **[Skill](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#bundled-skills).** Run a prompt repeatedly while the session stays open. Omit the interval and Claude self-paces between iterations. Omit the prompt and, [where available](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/scheduled-tasks#run-the-built-in-maintenance-prompt), Claude runs an autonomous maintenance check or the prompt in `.claude/loop.md`. Example: `/loop 5m check if the deploy finished`. See [Run prompts on a schedule](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/scheduled-tasks). Alias: `/proactive`
entries.searchText: Skill. Run a prompt repeatedly while the session stays open. Omit the interval and Claude self-paces between iterations. Omit the prompt and, where available, Claude runs an autonomous maintenance check or the prompt in .claude/loop.md. Example: /loop 5m check if the deploy finished. See Run prompts on a schedule. Alias: /proactive

entries.name: /mcp
entries.description: Manage MCP server connections and OAuth authentication. Run with no argument to open the interactive list, pass `reconnect <server>` to reconnect one disconnected server, or pass `enable`/`disable` with a server name or `all` to change connection state without opening the dialog
entries.searchText: Manage MCP server connections and OAuth authentication. Run with no argument to open the interactive list, pass reconnect <server to reconnect one disconnected server, or pass enable/disable with a server name or all to change connection state without opening the dialog

entries.name: /memory
entries.description: Edit `CLAUDE.md` memory files, enable or disable [auto-memory](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory#auto-memory), and view auto-memory entries
entries.searchText: Edit CLAUDE.md memory files, enable or disable auto-memory, and view auto-memory entries

entries.name: /mobile
entries.description: Show QR code to download the Claude mobile app. Aliases: `/ios`, `/android`
entries.searchText: Show QR code to download the Claude mobile app. Aliases: /ios, /android

entries.name: /model
entries.description: Switch the AI model and save it as your default for new sessions. For models that support it, use left/right arrows to [adjust effort level](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config#adjust-effort-level). With no argument, opens a picker; press `s` on a row to switch for the current session only. The picker asks for confirmation when the conversation has prior output, since the next response re-reads the full history without cached context. Once confirmed, the change applies without waiting for the current response to finish
entries.searchText: Switch the AI model and save it as your default for new sessions. For models that support it, use left/right arrows to adjust effort level. With no argument, opens a picker; press s on a row to switch for the current session only. The picker asks for confirmation when the conversation has prior output, since the next response re-reads the full history without cached context. Once confirmed, the change applies without waiting for the current response to finish

entries.name: /passes
entries.description: Share a free week of Claude Code with friends. Only visible if your account is eligible
entries.searchText: Share a free week of Claude Code with friends. Only visible if your account is eligible

entries.name: /permissions
entries.description: Manage allow, ask, and deny rules for tool permissions. Opens an interactive dialog where you can view rules by scope, add or remove rules, manage working directories, and review [recent auto mode denials](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/auto-mode-config#review-denials). Alias: `/allowed-tools`
entries.searchText: Manage allow, ask, and deny rules for tool permissions. Opens an interactive dialog where you can view rules by scope, add or remove rules, manage working directories, and review recent auto mode denials. Alias: /allowed-tools

entries.name: /plan
entries.description: Enter plan mode directly from the prompt. Pass an optional description to enter plan mode and immediately start with that task, for example `/plan fix the auth bug`
entries.searchText: Enter plan mode directly from the prompt. Pass an optional description to enter plan mode and immediately start with that task, for example /plan fix the auth bug

entries.name: /plugin
entries.description: Manage Claude Code [plugins](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins). Run with no argument to open the plugin menu, or pass a subcommand such as `list`, `install`, `enable`, or `disable` to act directly
entries.searchText: Manage Claude Code plugins. Run with no argument to open the plugin menu, or pass a subcommand such as list, install, enable, or disable to act directly

entries.name: /powerup
entries.description: Discover Claude Code features through quick interactive lessons with animated demos
entries.searchText: Discover Claude Code features through quick interactive lessons with animated demos

entries.name: /pr-comments
entries.description: Removed in v2.1.91. Ask Claude directly to view pull request comments instead. On earlier versions, fetches and displays comments from a GitHub pull request; automatically detects the PR for the current branch, or pass a PR URL or number. Requires the `gh` CLI
entries.searchText: Removed in v2.1.91. Ask Claude directly to view pull request comments instead. On earlier versions, fetches and displays comments from a GitHub pull request; automatically detects the PR for the current branch, or pass a PR URL or number. Requires the gh CLI

entries.name: /privacy-settings
entries.description: View and update your privacy settings. Only available for Pro and Max plan subscribers
entries.searchText: View and update your privacy settings. Only available for Pro and Max plan subscribers

entries.name: /radio
entries.description: Open Claude FM lo-fi radio in your browser. Prints the stream URL when no browser is available. Not available on Bedrock, Vertex, or Foundry
entries.searchText: Open Claude FM lo-fi radio in your browser. Prints the stream URL when no browser is available. Not available on Bedrock, Vertex, or Foundry

entries.name: /recap
entries.description: Generate a one-line summary of the current session on demand. See [Session recap](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/interactive-mode#session-recap) for the automatic recap that appears after you've been away
entries.searchText: Generate a one-line summary of the current session on demand. See Session recap for the automatic recap that appears after you've been away

entries.name: /release-notes
entries.description: View the changelog in an interactive version picker. Select a specific version to see its release notes, or choose to show all versions
entries.searchText: View the changelog in an interactive version picker. Select a specific version to see its release notes, or choose to show all versions

entries.name: /reload-plugins
entries.description: Reload all active [plugins](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins) to apply pending changes without restarting. Reports counts for each reloaded component and flags any load errors. When the reload would change which MCP tools are loaded and invalidate the prompt cache, the command warns and skips unless you pass `--force`
entries.searchText: Reload all active plugins to apply pending changes without restarting. Reports counts for each reloaded component and flags any load errors. When the reload would change which MCP tools are loaded and invalidate the prompt cache, the command warns and skips unless you pass --force

entries.name: /reload-skills
entries.description: Re-scan [skill](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills) and command directories so skills added or changed on disk during the session become available without restarting. Reports how many skills are available and how many were added or removed. Added in v2.1.152
entries.searchText: Re-scan skill and command directories so skills added or changed on disk during the session become available without restarting. Reports how many skills are available and how many were added or removed. Added in v2.1.152

entries.name: /remote-control
entries.description: Make this session available for [remote control](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control) from claude.ai. Alias: `/rc`
entries.searchText: Make this session available for remote control from claude.ai. Alias: /rc

entries.name: /remote-env
entries.description: Choose the default environment for [cloud agents](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web#configure-your-environment)
entries.searchText: Choose the default environment for cloud agents

entries.name: /rename
entries.description: Rename the current session and show the name on the prompt bar. Without a name, auto-generates one from conversation history
entries.searchText: Rename the current session and show the name on the prompt bar. Without a name, auto-generates one from conversation history

entries.name: /resume
entries.description: Resume a conversation by ID or name, or open the session picker. As of v2.1.144, [background sessions](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-view) appear in the picker marked with `bg`. Alias: `/continue`
entries.searchText: Resume a conversation by ID or name, or open the session picker. As of v2.1.144, background sessions appear in the picker marked with bg. Alias: /continue

entries.name: /review
entries.description: Review a pull request locally in your current session. For a deeper cloud-based review, see [`/code-review ultra`](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/ultrareview)
entries.searchText: Review a pull request locally in your current session. For a deeper cloud-based review, see /code-review ultra

entries.name: /rewind
entries.description: Rewind the conversation and/or code to a previous point, or summarize from a selected message. See [checkpointing](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/checkpointing). Aliases: `/checkpoint`, `/undo`
entries.searchText: Rewind the conversation and/or code to a previous point, or summarize from a selected message. See checkpointing. Aliases: /checkpoint, /undo

entries.name: /run
entries.description: **[Skill](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#bundled-skills).** Launch and drive your project's app to see a change working in the running app, not just in tests. See [Run and verify your app](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#run-and-verify-your-app). Requires Claude Code v2.1.145 or later
entries.searchText: Skill. Launch and drive your project's app to see a change working in the running app, not just in tests. See Run and verify your app. Requires Claude Code v2.1.145 or later

entries.name: /run-skill-generator
entries.description: **[Skill](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#bundled-skills).** Teach `/run` and `/verify` how to build, launch, and drive your project's app from a clean environment by writing a per-project [skill](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#run-and-verify-your-app). Requires Claude Code v2.1.145 or later
entries.searchText: Skill. Teach /run and /verify how to build, launch, and drive your project's app from a clean environment by writing a per-project skill. Requires Claude Code v2.1.145 or later

entries.name: /sandbox
entries.description: Toggle [sandbox mode](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sandboxing). Available on supported platforms only
entries.searchText: Toggle sandbox mode. Available on supported platforms only

entries.name: /schedule
entries.description: Create, update, list, or run [routines](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/routines), which execute on Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure. Claude walks you through the setup conversationally. Alias: `/routines`
entries.searchText: Create, update, list, or run routines, which execute on Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure. Claude walks you through the setup conversationally. Alias: /routines

entries.name: /scroll-speed
entries.description: Adjust mouse wheel [scroll speed](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/fullscreen#mouse-wheel-scrolling) interactively, with a ruler you can scroll while the dialog is open to preview the change. Available in [fullscreen rendering](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/fullscreen) only and not in the JetBrains IDE terminal
entries.searchText: Adjust mouse wheel scroll speed interactively, with a ruler you can scroll while the dialog is open to preview the change. Available in fullscreen rendering only and not in the JetBrains IDE terminal

entries.name: /security-review
entries.description: Analyze pending changes on the current branch for security vulnerabilities. Reviews the git diff and identifies risks like injection, auth issues, and data exposure
entries.searchText: Analyze pending changes on the current branch for security vulnerabilities. Reviews the git diff and identifies risks like injection, auth issues, and data exposure

entries.name: /setup-bedrock
entries.description: Configure [Amazon Bedrock](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/amazon-bedrock) authentication, region, and model pins through an interactive wizard. Only visible when `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK=1` is set. First-time Bedrock users can also access this wizard from the login screen
entries.searchText: Configure Amazon Bedrock authentication, region, and model pins through an interactive wizard. Only visible when CLAUDECODEUSEBEDROCK=1 is set. First-time Bedrock users can also access this wizard from the login screen

entries.name: /setup-vertex
entries.description: Configure [Google Vertex AI](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/google-vertex-ai) authentication, project, region, and model pins through an interactive wizard. Only visible when `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX=1` is set. First-time Vertex AI users can also access this wizard from the login screen
entries.searchText: Configure Google Vertex AI authentication, project, region, and model pins through an interactive wizard. Only visible when CLAUDECODEUSEVERTEX=1 is set. First-time Vertex AI users can also access this wizard from the login screen

entries.name: /simplify
entries.description: **[Skill](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#bundled-skills).** Review the changed code for cleanup opportunities and apply the fixes. Four review [agents](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents) run in parallel, covering reuse of existing helpers, simplification, efficiency, and whether the change sits at the right level of abstraction. From v2.1.154, the review does not look for correctness bugs. Use `/code-review` to find bugs. On earlier versions `/simplify` is equivalent to `/code-review --fix`. Pass a path or PR reference to review a specific target
entries.searchText: Skill. Review the changed code for cleanup opportunities and apply the fixes. Four review agents run in parallel, covering reuse of existing helpers, simplification, efficiency, and whether the change sits at the right level of abstraction. From v2.1.154, the review does not look for correctness bugs. Use /code-review to find bugs. On earlier versions /simplify is equivalent to /code-review --fix. Pass a path or PR reference to review a specific target

entries.name: /skills
entries.description: List available [skills](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills). Press `t` to sort by token count. Press `Space` to [hide a skill from Claude or the `/` menu](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#override-skill-visibility-from-settings), then `Enter` to save
entries.searchText: List available skills. Press t to sort by token count. Press Space to hide a skill from Claude or the / menu, then Enter to save

entries.name: /stats
entries.description: Alias for `/usage`. Opens on the Stats tab
entries.searchText: Alias for /usage. Opens on the Stats tab

entries.name: /status
entries.description: Open the Settings interface (Status tab) showing version, model, account, and connectivity. Works while Claude is responding, without waiting for the current response to finish
entries.searchText: Open the Settings interface (Status tab) showing version, model, account, and connectivity. Works while Claude is responding, without waiting for the current response to finish

entries.name: /statusline
entries.description: Configure Claude Code's [status line](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline). Describe what you want, or run without arguments to auto-configure from your shell prompt
entries.searchText: Configure Claude Code's status line. Describe what you want, or run without arguments to auto-configure from your shell prompt

entries.name: /stickers
entries.description: Order Claude Code stickers
entries.searchText: Order Claude Code stickers

entries.name: /stop
entries.description: Stop the current [background session](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-view). Only available while attached to a background session; the transcript and any worktree are kept. To detach without stopping, use `/exit` or press `←`
entries.searchText: Stop the current background session. Only available while attached to a background session; the transcript and any worktree are kept. To detach without stopping, use /exit or press ←

entries.name: /tasks
entries.description: View and manage everything running in the background. Also available as `/bashes`
entries.searchText: View and manage everything running in the background. Also available as /bashes

entries.name: /team-onboarding
entries.description: Generate a team onboarding guide from your Claude Code usage history. Claude analyzes your sessions, commands, and MCP server usage from the past 30 days and produces a markdown guide a teammate can paste as a first message to get set up quickly. For claude.ai subscribers on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans, also returns a share link teammates can open directly in Claude Code
entries.searchText: Generate a team onboarding guide from your Claude Code usage history. Claude analyzes your sessions, commands, and MCP server usage from the past 30 days and produces a markdown guide a teammate can paste as a first message to get set up quickly. For claude.ai subscribers on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans, also returns a share link teammates can open directly in Claude Code

entries.name: /teleport
entries.description: Pull a [Claude Code on the web](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web#from-web-to-terminal) session into this terminal: opens a picker, then fetches the branch and conversation. Also available as `/tp`. Requires a claude.ai subscription
entries.searchText: Pull a Claude Code on the web session into this terminal: opens a picker, then fetches the branch and conversation. Also available as /tp. Requires a claude.ai subscription

entries.name: /terminal-setup
entries.description: Configure terminal keybindings for Shift+Enter and other shortcuts. Only visible in terminals that need it, like VS Code, Cursor, Devin Desktop, Alacritty, or Zed
entries.searchText: Configure terminal keybindings for Shift+Enter and other shortcuts. Only visible in terminals that need it, like VS Code, Cursor, Devin Desktop, Alacritty, or Zed

entries.name: /theme
entries.description: Change the color theme. Includes an `auto` option that matches your terminal's light or dark background, light and dark variants, colorblind-accessible (daltonized) themes, ANSI themes that use your terminal's color palette, and any [custom themes](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/terminal-config#create-a-custom-theme) from `~/.claude/themes/` or plugins. Select **New custom theme…** to create one
entries.searchText: Change the color theme. Includes an auto option that matches your terminal's light or dark background, light and dark variants, colorblind-accessible (daltonized) themes, ANSI themes that use your terminal's color palette, and any custom themes from ~/.claude/themes/ or plugins. Select New custom theme… to create one

entries.name: /tui
entries.description: Set the terminal UI renderer and relaunch into it with your conversation intact. `fullscreen` enables the [flicker-free alt-screen renderer](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/fullscreen). With no argument, prints the active renderer
entries.searchText: Set the terminal UI renderer and relaunch into it with your conversation intact. fullscreen enables the flicker-free alt-screen renderer. With no argument, prints the active renderer

entries.name: /ultraplan
entries.description: Draft a plan in an [ultraplan](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/ultraplan) session, review it in your browser, then execute remotely or send it back to your terminal
entries.searchText: Draft a plan in an ultraplan session, review it in your browser, then execute remotely or send it back to your terminal

entries.name: /ultrareview
entries.description: Run a deep, multi-agent code review in a cloud sandbox with [ultrareview](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/ultrareview). The preferred invocation is now `/code-review ultra`, and `/ultrareview` remains as an alias. Includes 3 free runs on Pro and Max, then requires [usage credits](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/12429409-extra-usage-for-paid-claude-plans)
entries.searchText: Run a deep, multi-agent code review in a cloud sandbox with ultrareview. The preferred invocation is now /code-review ultra, and /ultrareview remains as an alias. Includes 3 free runs on Pro and Max, then requires usage credits

entries.name: /upgrade
entries.description: Open the upgrade page to switch to a higher plan tier
entries.searchText: Open the upgrade page to switch to a higher plan tier

entries.name: /usage
entries.description: Show session cost, plan usage limits, and activity stats. On a Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise plan, includes a breakdown of usage by skill, subagent, plugin, and MCP server. See the [cost tracking guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/costs#using-the-%2Fusage-command) for details. `/cost` and `/stats` are aliases
entries.searchText: Show session cost, plan usage limits, and activity stats. On a Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise plan, includes a breakdown of usage by skill, subagent, plugin, and MCP server. See the cost tracking guide for details. /cost and /stats are aliases

entries.name: /usage-credits
entries.description: Configure usage credits to keep working when you hit a limit. Previously `/extra-usage`
entries.searchText: Configure usage credits to keep working when you hit a limit. Previously /extra-usage

entries.name: /verify
entries.description: **[Skill](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#bundled-skills).** Confirm a code change does what it should by building your project's app, running it, and observing the result, rather than relying on tests or type checks. See [Run and verify your app](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills#run-and-verify-your-app). Requires Claude Code v2.1.145 or later
entries.searchText: Skill. Confirm a code change does what it should by building your project's app, running it, and observing the result, rather than relying on tests or type checks. See Run and verify your app. Requires Claude Code v2.1.145 or later

entries.name: /vim
entries.description: Removed in v2.1.92. To toggle between Vim and Normal editing modes, use `/config` → Editor mode
entries.searchText: Removed in v2.1.92. To toggle between Vim and Normal editing modes, use /config → Editor mode

entries.name: /voice
entries.description: Toggle [voice dictation](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/voice-dictation), or enable it in a specific mode. Requires a Claude.ai account
entries.searchText: Toggle voice dictation, or enable it in a specific mode. Requires a Claude.ai account

entries.name: /web-setup
entries.description: Connect your GitHub account to [Claude Code on the web](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/web-quickstart#connect-from-your-terminal) using your local `gh` CLI credentials. `/schedule` prompts for this automatically if GitHub isn't connected
entries.searchText: Connect your GitHub account to Claude Code on the web using your local gh CLI credentials. /schedule prompts for this automatically if GitHub isn't connected

entries.name: /workflows
entries.description: Open the [workflow](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/workflows#watch-the-run) progress view to watch, pause, resume, or save running and completed workflows
entries.searchText: Open the workflow progress view to watch, pause, resume, or save running and completed workflows
